code_description: SA plastic strips in rolls <= 20cm wide other
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for narrow self-adhesive plastic strips sold in rolls up to 20cm wide. These are PSA tapes and narrow-web labelstock used in industrial marking and bundling.
sales_interest: Tracks cross-border flows of narrow PSA tape and labelstock. Rising imports into a country signal growing local demand for industrial labels and tapes — potential to pitch converting solutions.

code_description: SA plastic sheets/films (excl. rolls <= 20cm), addition polymerisation — PE, PP, PVC-based labelstock
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for self-adhesive plastic films made from PE, PP, or PVC polymers, in wide rolls or sheets. This is the core filmic PSA labelstock category.
sales_interest: Core filmic labelstock trade. A country importing more of this material has growing demand for plastic labels — ideal for targeting converters and brand owners switching from paper to film.

code_description: SA plastic sheets/films (excl. rolls <= 20cm), other — catch-all for PSA labelstock
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for all other self-adhesive plastic films not classified elsewhere. Catch-all code covering specialty PSA labelstock such as PET-based, polyester, or multi-layer constructions.
sales_interest: Captures niche and specialty filmic labelstock trade. Growing volumes here signal demand for high-performance or specialty labels (durable, chemical-resistant, clear-on-clear) — opportunity for premium product pitches.

code_description: Self-adhesive paper and paperboard — paper PSA labelstock (face + adhesive + liner)
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for self-adhesive paper and paperboard. This is the single most important code for the PSA label industry — it covers complete labelstock rolls (face paper + adhesive + release liner) traded between countries.
sales_interest: The #1 series for PSA paper labelstock. Volume trends directly reflect label market size by country. Use to identify fast-growing markets, benchmark country performance, and spot where converters are sourcing material.

code_description: Gummed/adhesive paper & paperboard (excl. self-adhesive) — competing wet-gummed technology
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for non-self-adhesive gummed papers and paperboard. Covers wet-glue label papers and other adhesive-coated papers that compete with PSA technology.
sales_interest: Tracks the competing wet-glue label technology. Declining volumes here relative to 48114100 confirm the ongoing shift from wet-glue to PSA — useful data point when convincing customers to switch to self-adhesive.

code_description: Self-adhesive printed labels of paper/paperboard — finished converted PSA labels
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for finished printed self-adhesive labels made of paper or paperboard. These are converted labels ready to apply — the end product of the label converting chain.
sales_interest: Tracks finished PSA label trade between countries. Shows where label converting is concentrated vs where labels are imported. Useful to map the competitive landscape and identify countries reliant on label imports — potential converter prospects.

code_description: Other printed labels of paper/paperboard (excl. SA) — wet-glue, shrink sleeve labels
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for printed non-self-adhesive paper labels, including wet-glue labels, shrink sleeves, and wrap-around paper labels.
sales_interest: Tracks competing label technologies (wet-glue, shrink sleeve). Compare with 48211010 to show clients the PSA vs non-PSA market split. Declining non-PSA = opportunity to promote PSA conversion.

code_description: Self-adhesive labels paper/paperboard (unprinted) — blank thermal/shipping labels
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for unprinted (blank) self-adhesive paper labels. Covers blank thermal labels, shipping labels, and variable-information labels printed at point of use.
sales_interest: Tracks the fast-growing blank label segment driven by e-commerce shipping and thermal printing. Rising volumes indicate growth in logistics labelling — pitch thermal labelstock and blank label solutions.

code_description: Other labels paper/paperboard (unprinted, excl. SA) — blank non-PSA labels
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for unprinted non-self-adhesive paper labels, such as blank hang tags, cardboard tags, and insert labels.
sales_interest: Tracks non-PSA blank label trade. Niche but relevant — declining volumes here vs rising 48219010 shows the shift toward self-adhesive even in unprinted/industrial labels.

code_description: PE film, SG < 0.94, thickness <= 0.025mm — thin LDPE/LLDPE films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for very thin low-density polyethylene (LDPE/LLDPE) films under 25 microns. Used in flexible packaging and as a base substrate for some label constructions.
sales_interest: Thin PE film is a raw material for flexible packaging and some squeeze-tube labels. Rising trade signals growing flexible packaging demand — adjacent market where PSA labels may replace or complement.

code_description: PE film, SG < 0.94, thickness 0.025-0.05mm — medium LDPE films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for medium-gauge LDPE films (25-50 microns). Common in shrink wraps, bag films, and some label face stocks.
sales_interest: Medium PE film overlaps with label face stock gauges. Growing trade volumes can indicate expanding demand for PE-based labels (e.g. squeezable bottles) — pitch PE labelstock solutions.

code_description: PE film, SG < 0.94, thickness > 0.05mm — thicker LDPE films, label face stock gauge
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for thicker LDPE films above 50 microns. This gauge range is directly used as label face stock material for conformable container labels.
sales_interest: Directly relevant as label face stock material. This is the PE film gauge used for container labels. Track this to monitor raw material supply for PE label converters — and to benchmark pricing conversations.

code_description: Other PE film, SG < 0.94 — other LDPE/LLDPE films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other LDPE/LLDPE films not classified by thickness. Catch-all for specialty PE film grades.
sales_interest: Captures specialty PE film grades including those engineered for label applications. Monitor for emerging PE film innovations entering EU trade channels.

code_description: PE film, SG >= 0.94, thickness < 0.021mm — thin HDPE films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for very thin high-density polyethylene (HDPE) films under 21 microns. Used primarily in carrier bags and thin packaging wraps.
sales_interest: Thin HDPE film is mostly packaging, not labels. Low direct relevance, but useful as a benchmark for overall PE film market dynamics and pricing trends.

code_description: PE film, SG >= 0.94, thickness 0.021-0.160mm — standard HDPE films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for standard-gauge HDPE films (21-160 microns). Used in rigid containers and industrial packaging where label demand exists.
sales_interest: HDPE film at this gauge is used on rigid containers that need labels. Growing HDPE film trade suggests more rigid containers in circulation — more label opportunities for that substrate.

code_description: Other PE film, SG >= 0.94 — other HDPE films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other HDPE films not classified elsewhere. Includes specialty HDPE grades for industrial applications.
sales_interest: Niche HDPE films — minor direct impact on labels but contributes to overall PE market intelligence for pricing and supply negotiations.

code_description: BOPP film, thickness <= 0.10mm — the #1 filmic label face stock material
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for biaxially oriented polypropylene (BOPP) film up to 100 microns. BOPP is the single most important filmic label material — used for clear, white, and metallised label face stocks.
sales_interest: The most critical filmic raw material series. BOPP is the dominant film for beverage labels, personal care labels, and food labels. Track this to monitor supply, spot price trends, and identify where converters are sourcing.

code_description: Other PP film (cast/OPP), thickness <= 0.10mm — cast PP for wrap-around labels
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for cast or mono-oriented polypropylene films up to 100 microns. Used for wrap-around labels and flexible packaging.
sales_interest: Cast PP is used for wrap-around and in-mould labels. Growing trade here signals expansion of alternative labelling technologies — relevant for converters exploring beyond PSA.

code_description: PP film, thickness > 0.10mm — thicker PP films for heavy-duty applications
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for thick polypropylene films above 100 microns. Used in heavy-duty label applications and rigid packaging.
sales_interest: Thicker PP film serves industrial and heavy-duty label applications. Niche but high-value — track for specialty label opportunities in automotive, chemical drums, or outdoor signage.

code_description: Flexible PVC film (>= 6% plasticiser), not supported — conformable label/graphic film
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for flexible (plasticised) PVC film. Used in conformable labels, vehicle wraps, and graphic films that need to follow curved surfaces.
sales_interest: Flexible PVC is key for graphic films and vehicle wraps. Track for visual communication and signage label opportunities. Also relevant for wine capsules and conformable container labels.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other flexible PVC films not classified elsewhere. Includes specialty grades for niche label and graphic applications.
sales_interest: Captures specialty flexible PVC grades. Monitor alongside 39204310 for a complete picture of the PVC graphic and label film market.

code_description: Rigid PVC film thickness > 1mm — thick rigid PVC for blister packaging
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for thick rigid PVC sheets above 1mm. Primarily used in blister packaging for pharma and consumer goods.
sales_interest: Rigid PVC blister packs always carry labels. Growing blister packaging trade means more label demand in pharma and consumer electronics packaging.

code_description: Other rigid PVC film — thin rigid PVC
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other rigid PVC films. Includes thinner rigid PVC used in shrink sleeves and thermoformed packaging.
sales_interest: Thin rigid PVC is used in shrink sleeve labels — a competing technology to PSA. Track this to monitor the PSA vs shrink sleeve balance in beverage and personal care labelling.

code_description: PET film, thickness 0.025-0.35mm — standard PET for label face stocks and release liners
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for standard-gauge PET (polyester) film between 25 and 350 microns. PET film is used both as a label face stock (clear, durable labels) and as a release liner substrate.
sales_interest: Highly relevant — PET is used for both label faces and release liners. Track to monitor raw material supply for the entire PSA label chain. Price movements here directly impact labelstock costs.

code_description: PET film, thickness > 0.35mm — heavy PET sheets for industrial labels
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for thick PET film above 350 microns. Used in heavy-duty industrial labels, rating plates, and durable identification products.
sales_interest: Thick PET serves the industrial durable label market — rating plates, asset tags, outdoor labels. Niche but high margin. Track for specialty label sales opportunities.

code_description: Polycarbonate film — durable label substrates for harsh environments
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for polycarbonate film. Extremely durable and heat-resistant, used for labels in harsh environments (automotive, electronics, industrial equipment).
sales_interest: Polycarbonate labels serve automotive and electronics OEMs. Very high-value niche. Track to spot growth in durable label demand and target industrial customers.

code_description: Other polyester film (PEN, PBT, etc.) — specialty high-performance label substrates
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for specialty polyester films like PEN and PBT. High-performance materials used where standard PET is insufficient — extreme temperatures, chemical resistance, or dimensional stability.
sales_interest: Ultra-specialty label substrates. Small volumes but very high margins. Growing trade here signals expanding technical label applications in aerospace, electronics, or EV batteries.

code_description: Polyimide film — high-temperature labels for electronics and automotive
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for polyimide (Kapton-type) film. Withstands extreme temperatures (up to 400C) and is used for labels on circuit boards, engine parts, and soldering processes.
sales_interest: Polyimide labels are essential in electronics manufacturing (PCB labels) and automotive (under-hood labels). Track to monitor growth in electronic component labelling — especially relevant with EV expansion.

code_description: Other plastic film, n.e.c. — catch-all including bio-based and TPU films
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for all other plastic films not classified elsewhere. Includes emerging materials like bio-based PLA films, TPU films, and other novel substrates.
sales_interest: Captures emerging and sustainable film materials. Growing volumes here may signal uptake of bio-based or recyclable label substrates — important for sustainability-driven sales pitches.

code_description: Adhesives retail sale net weight <= 1kg — small-format retail adhesives
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for small consumer-packaged adhesives under 1kg. Primarily retail glue products, not industrial.
sales_interest: Low direct relevance to industrial label sales, but provides context on the broader adhesives market and consumer DIY trends.

code_description: Water-based adhesives from synthetic polymers — water-based PSA emulsions, PVAc
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for water-based synthetic adhesives. This category includes acrylic emulsion PSA — the dominant adhesive technology in the label industry — as well as PVA and other water-based formulations.
sales_interest: Core adhesive for PSA labelstock manufacturing. Water-based acrylic is the most widely used label adhesive. Track to monitor the health and pricing of the adhesive supply chain — directly impacts labelstock cost.

code_description: Other adhesives based on synthetic polymers or rubber — solvent-based PSA, hot-melt, UV-curable
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for non-water-based synthetic adhesives. Covers solvent-based PSA, hot-melt PSA, UV-curable adhesives, and rubber-based formulations used in labels and tapes.
sales_interest: Covers hot-melt and solvent-based PSA adhesives — the alternative technologies to water-based. Hot-melt is growing fast in label applications. Track to benchmark adhesive technology shifts and supply trends.

code_description: Other prepared glues/adhesives n.e.c. — other industrial adhesives
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other industrial adhesives not classified elsewhere. Includes specialty formulations for niche bonding applications.
sales_interest: Broad industrial adhesive category. Provides context on overall adhesive market health — useful as background intelligence when discussing adhesive pricing with labelstock suppliers.

code_description: Silicones in primary forms — release coating silicones for liner substrates
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for silicone polymers in primary (raw) forms. Silicones are essential for release coating on label liners — every PSA label roll has a silicone-coated liner that allows clean dispensing.
sales_interest: Critical raw material. Every PSA label needs silicone-coated liner. Silicone supply and pricing directly impact labelstock costs. Track for early signals of supply disruption or price changes to discuss with customers.

code_description: Glassine papers in rolls or sheets — primary release liner base paper
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for glassine paper. Glassine is the primary base paper for PSA label release liners — a super-calendered, dense, smooth paper that holds silicone coatings.
sales_interest: Core liner material. Glassine is used in the majority of PSA label constructions. Supply constraints here directly create labelstock shortages. Track to anticipate availability issues and price movements.

code_description: Other glazed transparent/translucent papers — specialty transparent liner papers
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other glazed or translucent papers. Includes specialty liner base papers and alternative transparent papers used in niche release liner constructions.
sales_interest: Specialty liner papers for high-end label applications. Track alongside glassine to get the full picture of liner substrate supply.

code_description: Black printing ink — black inks for flexo, offset, digital label printing
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for black printing ink. Used across all label printing processes — flexographic, offset, letterpress, and digital.
sales_interest: Printing ink is a core consumable for label converters. Track to monitor converter activity levels and input cost trends — converters buying more ink are printing more labels.

code_description: Other printing ink — all colour inks for label printing
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for all non-black printing inks. Covers the full colour range (CMYK + spot colours) used in label printing across all processes.
sales_interest: Colour ink is the larger ink category for labels. Rising ink trade = rising label print volumes. Use alongside black ink to estimate overall label converting activity by country.

code_description: RFID tags, inlays, proximity cards — RFID smart label inlays
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for RFID tags and inlays. These are the electronic components embedded in smart labels for item-level tracking in retail, logistics, and supply chain management.
sales_interest: Fastest-growing label segment. RFID inlay trade volumes directly show smart label adoption rates. Use to identify countries and industries deploying RFID — pitch label converting partnerships for smart labels.

code_description: Other semiconductor media — other smart label components
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for other semiconductor-based storage media. Includes NFC chips, other IC components, and smart label elements beyond standard RFID inlays.
sales_interest: Captures next-gen smart label components (NFC, sensor tags). Track for emerging smart packaging opportunities beyond standard RFID — luxury brand authentication, pharma temperature monitoring.

code_description: Stamping foils — hot stamping foils for premium label embellishment
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for stamping foils. Hot stamping and cold foil embellishments add metallic, holographic, or coloured effects to premium labels for wine, spirits, cosmetics, and luxury goods.
sales_interest: Foil consumption is a direct proxy for premium label demand. Rising foil trade = more luxury labelling activity. Use to target wine, spirits, and cosmetics brands investing in premium packaging.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for fruit juice products traded between countries. Every juice bottle, carton, or pouch carries at least one label.
sales_interest: Fruit juice is a steady label consumer — each unit needs a label. Track trade flows to spot which countries have growing juice markets and may need more label supply.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for mineral and sparkling water. Water is one of the highest-volume beverage categories, and every bottle carries a PSA or sleeve label.
sales_interest: Massive label volumes — water bottles are among the most labelled items globally. Track to identify countries with growing water consumption and pitch label solutions to local bottlers.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for soft drinks, energy drinks, teas, and other non-alcoholic beverages. High-innovation category with frequent packaging redesigns.
sales_interest: High-value label market — frequent SKU changes and promotional labels. Growing trade signals opportunities to supply labels for new product launches and seasonal campaigns.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for beer. Beer uses a mix of PSA labels, wet-glue labels, and sleeves, with craft beer driving growth in short-run, high-quality labels.
sales_interest: Beer labelling is a major market, especially with craft beer growth driving demand for premium short-run labels. Track to target breweries and identify PSA conversion opportunities from wet-glue.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for wine. Wine labels are among the most premium in the label industry — high design, embossing, foil stamping, and textured substrates.
sales_interest: Premium label segment. Wine labelling demands highest quality materials and converting. Track wine trade to spot growth markets for premium labelstock, foils, and textured papers.

code_description: Spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for spirits and liqueurs. Ultra-premium labels with embellishments — the highest spend-per-label in the beverage sector.
sales_interest: Top-tier premium labels. Spirits brands spend more per label than any other beverage category. Growing trade = growing opportunity for premium labelstock, foil, and embellishment sales.

code_description: Beauty, make-up and skin care preparations
description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for cosmetics and skincare products. Every cream, lotion, and makeup item carries a label — often with premium finishes and complex constructions.
sales_interest: Cosmetics demand some of the most sophisticated labels — clear-on-clear, multi-layer, textured, metallic. Growing trade here means more demand for premium label solutions. High-margin opportunity.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for shampoos, conditioners, and hair care products. Labels on curved bottles with moisture exposure require durable adhesives and films.
sales_interest: Hair care products need moisture-resistant labels on squeezable and curved containers — great fit for filmic PSA labels. Track to target personal care brands expanding in EU markets.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for personal hygiene products including shaving products, deodorants, and bath preparations. Labels face moisture and handling stress.
sales_interest: Personal care labelling requires durability and premium aesthetics. Growing trade supports demand for film labels and specialty adhesives that resist bathroom environments.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for household cleaning products, detergents, and surface cleaners. High-volume labelling with chemical resistance requirements.
sales_interest: High-volume label market. Cleaning products need chemical-resistant labels (exposure to product spills). Growing trade means more demand for durable filmic labels and aggressive adhesives.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for pharmaceutical products in measured doses (tablets, capsules, injectables). Every medicine package requires regulatory labelling, often with security and serialisation features.
sales_interest: Pharma is a high-compliance, high-value label market. Every medication needs labels with regulatory text, barcodes, and often anti-counterfeit features. Growing pharma trade = growing demand for security labels, booklet labels, and track-and-trace solutions.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for prepared and preserved meat products (ham, sausages, canned meat). All require food-grade labelling with nutritional and regulatory information.
sales_interest: Meat products need food-safe labels that resist cold, moisture, and grease. Growing trade signals demand for freezer-grade and chilled-food labels — pitch specialised adhesives and substrates.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for prepared and preserved fish (canned fish, smoked salmon, surimi). Labels must withstand refrigeration and moisture.
sales_interest: Similar to meat — cold-chain labels needed. Fish products often use premium labels for branded smoked and fresh products. Track to target seafood producers with appropriate label solutions.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for prepared and preserved vegetables (canned, frozen, pickled). High-volume commodity labelling market.
sales_interest: High-volume, cost-sensitive label market. Canned and frozen vegetables use billions of labels per year. Track trade volumes to benchmark the core food label market size by country.

description: Monthly EU trade data (euros and volumes) for miscellaneous food preparations including sauces, seasonings, protein bars, and meal replacements. Fast-growing innovation-driven segment.
sales_interest: Fast-moving category with frequent product launches and packaging redesigns. New food brands = new label contracts. Track to spot innovation-driven label demand growth.

description: Monthly index tracking how much the EU paper and paper products industry is producing. Base year 2021 = 100. Covers all paper manufacturing including the paper used for labels, liners, and packaging.
sales_interest: Directly tracks production in our supply chain. Falling production means potential paper shortages or price increases for labelstock. Rising production signals healthy supply — use to reassure customers on material availability.

description: Monthly index tracking production volume in the pulp, paper, and paperboard sub-sector. This is upstream of label manufacturing — the mills producing the base papers.
sales_interest: Upstream indicator. If pulp and paper mills are cutting production, expect tighter supply and higher prices for label face papers and liner papers downstream. Early warning signal.

description: Monthly index tracking production of paper and paperboard specifically (excluding pulp). Measures the output of paper mills that produce the sheets and rolls feeding the label chain.
sales_interest: Direct measure of paper mill output. Track month-over-month to anticipate supply conditions for paper labelstock and release liner base papers.

description: Monthly index tracking production of converted paper products — packaging, stationery, and paper-based articles including labels. Shows overall converting industry activity.
sales_interest: Covers the paper converting sector which includes label converters. Rising production = more label output. Falling = converters may be struggling — potential to offer competitive supply deals.

code_description: Other articles of paper and paperboard
description: Monthly index tracking production of other converted paper products not elsewhere classified. Includes finished labels, adhesive tapes, and specialty paper products.
sales_interest: Closest NACE code to the label converting industry itself. Track this as a direct proxy for label manufacturing volumes in the EU.

description: Monthly index tracking production output of the EU printing industry. Label printing is a major segment — flexographic, digital, offset, and letterpress label printing activity.
sales_interest: Label converters are printers. This index reflects their production volumes. Falling printing output = converters have spare capacity and may be more receptive to new business or switching suppliers.

description: Monthly index tracking production in the broader chemicals sector across the EU. Includes adhesives, resins, solvents, and coating chemicals used in labelstock manufacturing.
sales_interest: Broad chemicals production affects the entire label supply chain — adhesives, coatings, solvents. Track for early signals of chemical supply disruptions that could impact labelstock production.

description: Monthly index tracking production of paints, varnishes, printing inks, and mastics. Printing ink production directly correlates with label and packaging print activity.
sales_interest: Printing ink production is a proxy for printing volume. More ink produced = more labels being printed. Use to validate label market growth claims in customer conversations.

description: Monthly index tracking production of adhesives and glues. Includes PSA emulsions, hot-melt adhesives, and industrial adhesives — the bonding layer in every self-adhesive label.
sales_interest: Direct supply chain indicator. PSA adhesive production levels determine how much labelstock can be coated. Supply dips here signal potential labelstock shortages. Critical for supply planning conversations.

description: Monthly index tracking production in the rubber and plastics sector. Includes plastic film production, PSA tapes, and all converted plastic products relevant to labels.
sales_interest: Broad indicator covering plastic film and tape production. Rising output means more filmic material available for label face stocks and packaging — healthy supply conditions.

description: Monthly index tracking production of plastics products (excluding rubber). Covers plastic film, sheets, tubes, and all converted plastic items.
sales_interest: More focused plastics indicator. Tracks overall plastic converting activity which includes filmic label and tape production.

description: Monthly index tracking production of plastic plates, sheets, tubes, and profiles. This is where film extrusion happens — the production of PE, PP, PET, and PVC films used in labels.
sales_interest: Very relevant — this is the film extrusion sector producing raw filmic label substrates. Production trends here directly impact filmic labelstock availability and pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking production of other plastic products not elsewhere classified. Includes PSA tapes, self-adhesive film products, and specialty plastic items.
sales_interest: Includes PSA tape manufacturing. Track as a supplementary indicator for self-adhesive product production volumes.

description: Monthly index tracking production of general-purpose machinery including labelling machines, packaging equipment, and converting machinery.
sales_interest: Labelling machine production signals capital investment in labelling lines. Rising = customers investing in new labelling capacity. Use to identify companies expanding and likely needing more label supply.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue (domestic sales + exports) of EU paper and paper products manufacturers. Shows whether the sector is growing or shrinking in euro terms.
sales_interest: Revenue health of paper manufacturers. Growing turnover means stable supplier base. Declining turnover may lead to mill closures or consolidation — risk to label paper supply.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU printing companies. Reflects the financial health of the label converting industry and overall commercial printing.
sales_interest: Revenue health of label converters and printers — our direct customers. Growing turnover means they can invest and grow. Struggling turnover means they may be open to switching to more cost-effective suppliers.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU chemical manufacturers. Includes revenue from adhesives, coatings, and specialty chemicals used in label production.
sales_interest: Revenue health of our chemical suppliers. Strong turnover = stable supply chain. Weak turnover may signal supplier financial stress or potential for negotiation.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU rubber and plastics manufacturers. Includes revenue from film producers, plastic converters, and tape manufacturers.
sales_interest: Revenue health of the plastics supply chain. Track to assess financial stability of film suppliers and plastic converters.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU paper manufacturers. Shows how much of their output is sold locally versus exported.
sales_interest: Domestic paper sales trends. If domestic turnover falls while exports rise, local paper prices may increase as supply shifts abroad. Relevant for label buyers sourcing locally.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU printers and converters. Reflects local label market demand.
sales_interest: Local printing demand. If domestic turnover is falling, converters may have excess capacity — good time to pitch new business. Rising = healthy local market.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU chemical manufacturers. Reflects local demand for industrial chemicals including adhesives.
sales_interest: Domestic chemical demand. Falling domestic turnover could mean chemical companies prioritise exports, tightening local supply for label manufacturers.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU rubber and plastics manufacturers. Reflects local demand for plastic products.
sales_interest: Local plastics demand. Track to understand whether film and tape producers are seeing domestic growth — supports healthy local supply chains.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU paper manufacturers. Shows how much paper production is being sold outside the producing country.
sales_interest: Paper export trends. Rising export turnover means EU paper is competitive globally but may tighten local supply. Useful for understanding supply dynamics.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU printers. Shows cross-border label and print sales activity.
sales_interest: Export activity of label converters. Rising exports = converters serving foreign markets. Interesting to identify countries that are net label importers — potential market entry points.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU chemical manufacturers. Reflects international demand for EU-made chemicals.
sales_interest: Chemical export trends. If EU adhesive/chemical exports are rising, local supply may tighten. Useful for planning sourcing strategies with customers.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU rubber and plastics manufacturers. Reflects international competitiveness of EU film producers.
sales_interest: Film and plastics export trends. Rising exports may reduce local availability. Track to inform customers about potential supply dynamics.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of paper and paper products (domestic + export combined). Shows whether paper prices are rising or falling across the EU.
sales_interest: Direct signal on paper cost trends. Rising prices here flow straight into labelstock pricing. Use to justify price increases to customers or to show pricing stability.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of pulp, paper, and paperboard. Upstream pricing that determines the cost base for label papers.
sales_interest: Upstream paper pricing. Price changes at pulp/paper level take 1-3 months to flow into labelstock. Use as an early indicator to prepare customers for coming price adjustments.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of paper and paperboard. The direct cost input for label face papers and liner base papers.
sales_interest: Most relevant paper price index for labels. Track month-by-month to build the pricing narrative with customers — showing that input costs justify your pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of converted paper products. Reflects the selling prices of paper-based products including labels.
sales_interest: Converted paper product prices — includes label selling prices. Use to benchmark whether your label prices are in line with the market trend.

code_description: Other articles of paper and paperboard
description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of other converted paper articles including labels and tapes. The closest price index to finished label products.
sales_interest: Closest price proxy for finished label products. Use as a market benchmark when negotiating with customers — shows where the overall market is trending on price.

description: Monthly index tracking selling prices charged by EU printers. Reflects the pricing environment for print and label converting services.
sales_interest: What printers and converters are charging. Rising prices = healthy pricing power for converters. Falling = competitive pressure and potential margin squeeze in the converting market.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of chemicals. Affects the cost of adhesives, coatings, and resins used in labelstock.
sales_interest: Chemical price trends impact adhesive and coating costs. Rising chemical prices put pressure on labelstock margins. Use to explain cost dynamics to customers.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of inks, varnishes, and coatings. Directly reflects what label converters pay for their printing consumables.
sales_interest: Ink and coating price trends for label converters. Rising prices here squeeze converter margins and may push them to raise finished label prices.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of adhesives and glues. This directly reflects PSA adhesive pricing — a major cost component in labelstock.
sales_interest: One of the most important price indices. Adhesive costs are a major driver of labelstock pricing. Track to anticipate cost changes and build pricing arguments.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of rubber and plastic products. Reflects overall plastics pricing including films and tapes.
sales_interest: Broad plastics pricing trend. Rising prices signal input cost pressure across the filmic label supply chain.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of plastics products. Covers pricing trends for plastic films, sheets, and converted plastic items.
sales_interest: Plastics product prices — more focused than C22. Track for film and tape pricing trends relevant to filmic labelstock.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of plastic films, sheets, and profiles. This is the price of extruded films — PE, PP, PET, PVC — at the factory gate.
sales_interest: Highly relevant film pricing index. Tracks what film extruders charge for their output — the raw material cost for filmic labelstock. Use to justify filmic label pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of other plastic products including tapes and self-adhesive films.
sales_interest: Tracks pricing for tapes and specialty plastic products. Useful as a benchmark for PSA tape and specialty label pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking selling prices of general-purpose machinery including labelling and packaging equipment.
sales_interest: Labelling machine prices. Rising prices = higher capex for customers investing in labelling lines. Relevant when selling label solutions that include equipment recommendations.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of paper products. Shows what local paper buyers pay, separated from export pricing effects.
sales_interest: Domestic paper prices — what your local labelstock suppliers actually pay for paper. May diverge from export prices due to contract structures.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of pulp, paper, and paperboard within each EU country.
sales_interest: Local pulp/paper prices. Track to understand regional cost differences — useful for multi-country label supply deals.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of paper and paperboard. The local cost of paper that feeds into domestically produced labelstock.
sales_interest: Local paper cost benchmark. Compare with total prices to see if domestic buyers pay more or less than the blended domestic+export average.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of converted paper products. Reflects local market pricing for paper-based goods including labels.
sales_interest: Domestic converted paper prices — what labels and paper products sell for locally. Benchmark your pricing against the market trend.

code_description: Other articles of paper and paperboard
description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of other paper articles including labels and tapes.
sales_interest: Most relevant domestic label price benchmark. Use to check if your domestic pricing is aligned with the overall market.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices charged by printers and converters for their local market.
sales_interest: What converters charge domestically. Use to understand local printing market pricing pressure — relevant if you sell labelstock to local converters.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of chemicals. Shows local chemical pricing dynamics relevant to adhesive and coating costs.
sales_interest: Local chemical prices. Compare with non-domestic prices to see if importing chemicals might be cheaper — relevant for labelstock manufacturers sourcing adhesives.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of inks, varnishes, and coatings in each EU market.
sales_interest: Local ink and coating prices — what converters pay for their printing consumables domestically.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of adhesives. Reflects what local adhesive buyers — including labelstock coaters — pay.
sales_interest: Local adhesive prices. Critical for labelstock manufacturers buying adhesive domestically. Rising local adhesive prices directly squeeze labelstock margins.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of rubber and plastic products within each EU country.
sales_interest: Local plastics prices. Track for understanding regional cost structures in the filmic label supply chain.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of plastics products.
sales_interest: Focused local plastics pricing. Relevant for converters sourcing film domestically.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of plastic films, sheets, and profiles. The local cost of extruded films.
sales_interest: Local film extrusion prices. What label converters pay for domestically produced PE, PP, and PET films. Core input cost indicator.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of other plastic products including tapes and adhesive films.
sales_interest: Local tape and specialty plastic pricing. Benchmark for domestic PSA tape and film products.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of general-purpose machinery including labelling equipment.
sales_interest: Local machinery prices. What labelling machine buyers pay in their domestic market.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU paper products. Shows what paper manufacturers charge for exports.
sales_interest: Export paper pricing. Compare with domestic prices to spot arbitrage or to understand pricing when sourcing paper cross-border within the EU.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of pulp, paper, and paperboard.
sales_interest: Export prices for upstream paper. Useful for multi-country sourcing strategies.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of paper and paperboard. What paper mills charge for cross-border sales.
sales_interest: Paper export prices — relevant if sourcing label papers from mills in other EU countries. Compare with domestic prices to find best-value sourcing.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of converted paper products. Reflects cross-border competitiveness of paper converters.
sales_interest: Export pricing of converted paper products including labels. Shows cross-border competitiveness — relevant for pan-European label supply contracts.

code_description: Other articles of paper and paperboard
description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of other paper articles including labels.
sales_interest: Export label pricing benchmark. Useful for understanding competitive pricing in cross-border label sales.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU printing services. Reflects cross-border print/label converting pricing.
sales_interest: Export printing prices. Shows what converters charge for cross-border work — relevant for understanding pan-European label market pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU chemicals. Reflects international pricing for adhesives and chemical inputs.
sales_interest: Export chemical prices. If EU chemicals are priced higher for export, domestic buyers may benefit from relatively better pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of inks, varnishes, and coatings.
sales_interest: Export ink and coating prices. Relevant for converters sourcing printing consumables cross-border.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of adhesives. Reflects what EU adhesive manufacturers charge for cross-border sales.
sales_interest: Export adhesive prices. Compare with domestic to assess cross-border sourcing opportunities for labelstock manufacturers.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of rubber and plastic products.
sales_interest: Export plastics pricing. Useful for understanding cross-border film sourcing dynamics.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of plastics products.
sales_interest: Export plastics product pricing. Relevant for sourcing films from other EU countries.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of plastic films, sheets, and profiles.
sales_interest: Export film prices. What extruders charge for cross-border film sales — relevant for multi-country filmic labelstock sourcing.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of other plastic products including tapes.
sales_interest: Export pricing for tapes and specialty plastics. Niche but relevant for cross-border tape supply.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of general-purpose machinery.
sales_interest: Export machinery prices. Relevant when advising customers on labelling equipment purchases from other EU countries.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported paper products entering the EU. Shows whether imported paper is getting cheaper or more expensive.
sales_interest: Import paper costs. Rising import prices may push labelstock manufacturers to source domestically — or absorb higher costs. Key for understanding paper supply cost dynamics.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported pulp, paper, and paperboard. Reflects global paper market pricing flowing into Europe.
sales_interest: Imported pulp/paper costs. Global paper price movements hit EU label costs with a lag. Early signal for upcoming paper-related price adjustments.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported paper and paperboard. The price of paper entering the EU from global markets.
sales_interest: Imported paper cost benchmark. Relevant when EU label paper supply is tight and alternatives must be sourced from outside Europe.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported converted paper products. Includes imported labels and paper-based packaging.
sales_interest: Cost of imported converted paper goods. Rising import prices may protect local converters from foreign competition — good news for EU-based label companies.

code_description: Other articles of paper and paperboard
description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported other paper articles including labels.
sales_interest: Import price of finished labels entering the EU. Rising = less competitive imports, protecting local converters. Falling = more competitive imports threatening local market.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported printing services and products entering the EU.
sales_interest: Import cost of printed products. Tracks whether offshore label printing is getting cheaper or more expensive compared to local EU converting.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported chemicals entering the EU. Affects the cost base for adhesives, coatings, and resins.
sales_interest: Imported chemical costs. Many adhesive raw materials are globally sourced. Rising import prices flow into adhesive costs and ultimately labelstock pricing.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported inks, varnishes, and coatings entering the EU.
sales_interest: Import cost of inks and coatings. Relevant for converters sourcing printing consumables from outside the EU.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported adhesives entering the EU. Reflects global PSA adhesive pricing.
sales_interest: Imported adhesive costs. Critical if labelstock manufacturers source PSA from outside Europe. Rising import prices may push toward local sourcing.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported rubber and plastic products entering the EU.
sales_interest: Import cost of plastics. Reflects global plastic pricing flowing into EU film and label supply chains.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported plastics products entering the EU.
sales_interest: Imported plastics product costs. Relevant for converters sourcing films or plastic components from outside Europe.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported plastic films, sheets, and profiles. The import cost of extruded films from outside the EU.
sales_interest: Import cost of films — PE, PP, PET from global suppliers. Directly relevant when film prices are tight and converters look to import. Tracks competitiveness of non-EU film producers.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported other plastic products including tapes and adhesive films.
sales_interest: Import cost of specialty plastics and tapes. Niche but relevant for PSA tape import dynamics.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported machinery entering the EU. Includes labelling and packaging equipment from Asia and other regions.
sales_interest: Import cost of machinery. Relevant when customers evaluate imported vs EU-made labelling machines — lower import costs may drive capex investment in new labelling lines.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in the EU paper industry. A proxy for production capacity utilisation and workforce engagement.
sales_interest: Workforce activity at paper mills. Falling hours signal production cuts — potential supply tightening. Rising hours = mills running harder, healthy supply. Use as an early indicator.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in the EU printing industry. Reflects the operational intensity of label converters and printers.
sales_interest: Workforce activity at converters and printers. Falling labour hours = converters slowing down and potentially open to new business. Rising = busy and growing.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in the EU chemicals sector. Reflects workforce activity at chemical plants producing adhesives and coatings.
sales_interest: Chemical sector workforce trends. Labour reductions at chemical plants may foreshadow supply disruptions for adhesives and coatings.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in the EU rubber and plastics sector. Proxy for production capacity in the film and plastic converting industry.
sales_interest: Plastics sector workforce. Track to understand capacity utilisation — if the sector is adding hours, film production capacity is being maximised.

description: Monthly survey of EU paper industry managers asking about recent production, order books, and expectations. Score above zero = optimism, below zero = pessimism.
sales_interest: Forward-looking sentiment from paper suppliers. If paper industry confidence is dropping, expect defensive behaviour — possible price hikes or production cuts. Prepare customers accordingly.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from pulp and paper mill managers across the EU. Measures sentiment about production trends, orders, and outlook.
sales_interest: Upstream supplier sentiment. Pessimistic paper mills may reduce investment and tighten supply. Optimistic mills signal stable supply ahead.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from paper and paperboard producers. Reflects their view on current conditions and near-term outlook.
sales_interest: Paper mill confidence. Direct reading of how our paper suppliers feel about the market — use to anticipate their pricing and capacity decisions.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from paper converting companies. Reflects their sentiment on orders, stocks, and future production.
sales_interest: Converter confidence. Optimistic converters are investing and growing — good time to pitch new materials. Pessimistic converters may be more price-sensitive.

code_description: Other articles of paper and paperboard
description: Monthly confidence indicator from manufacturers of other paper products including labels and tapes.
sales_interest: Closest sentiment indicator to the label industry itself. Reflects how label and tape manufacturers feel about the near-term outlook.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from printing industry managers. Captures their views on order books, capacity utilisation, and expectations.
sales_interest: Printer and converter confidence. Low confidence = they may be struggling and open to supplier changes. High confidence = healthy market with potential for volume growth.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from chemical industry managers. Reflects broad chemical sector sentiment on production and demand.
sales_interest: Chemical industry sentiment. Pessimism here can signal upcoming raw material disruptions. Optimism suggests stable adhesive and coating supply.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from manufacturers of inks, paints, and coatings. Reflects their view on demand and business conditions.
sales_interest: Ink and coatings supplier sentiment. Their confidence reflects the health of the printing and converting market they supply.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from adhesive manufacturers. Directly reflects how PSA adhesive producers view market conditions.
sales_interest: Adhesive maker confidence — directly relevant. If adhesive producers are pessimistic, they may be seeing order slowdowns from labelstock coaters. Key leading indicator.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from rubber and plastics industry managers.
sales_interest: Plastics sector sentiment. Broad signal on the health of the film and tape supply chain.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from plastics product manufacturers.
sales_interest: Focused plastics sentiment. Track for film and plastic converting industry outlook.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from manufacturers of plastic films, sheets, and profiles — the film extruders.
sales_interest: Film extruder confidence. Directly reflects how label film suppliers view their market. Use to anticipate film supply and pricing behaviour.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from manufacturers of other plastic products including tapes and adhesive films.
sales_interest: Tape and specialty plastics confidence. Supplementary sentiment indicator for the self-adhesive products market.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from general-purpose machinery manufacturers including labelling and packaging equipment makers.
sales_interest: Machinery maker confidence reflects customer investment appetite. High confidence = labelling machine orders are strong, meaning end-users are investing in new lines.

description: Monthly index tracking how much the EU food manufacturing industry is producing. Food is the single largest end-market for labels — every packaged food item needs at least one label.
sales_interest: The #1 demand indicator for labels. More food production = more labels needed. Track to identify growth markets and to demonstrate label demand drivers to customers. Essential for forecasting.

description: Monthly index tracking EU beverage production volumes. Beverages are the second-largest label end-market — bottles, cans, and cartons all carry labels.
sales_interest: Major label demand driver. Rising beverage production = rising demand for bottle labels, can wraps, and multipack labels. Track to target beverage companies expanding production.

description: Monthly index tracking EU tobacco product manufacturing. Tobacco labelling is highly regulated with frequent design changes due to health warning requirements.
sales_interest: Regulatory-driven label market. Tobacco labels change frequently due to legislation. Even stable production means recurring label demand. Track for compliance-driven labelling opportunities.

description: Monthly index tracking EU production of soaps, detergents, cosmetics, and toiletries. This sector uses premium labels with sophisticated designs and materials.
sales_interest: Premium label demand. HPC brands spend heavily on label design and materials. Rising production = growing demand for high-quality filmic labels, metallised substrates, and special finishes.

description: Monthly index tracking EU pharmaceutical production. Pharma requires labels with strict compliance, serialisation, and often anti-counterfeit features.
sales_interest: High-value, compliance-driven label demand. Pharma production growth = more demand for booklet labels, security features, and serialised labels. Less price-sensitive, more quality-driven market.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU food manufacturers. Reflects the financial health and spending power of the largest label-buying sector.
sales_interest: Revenue health of our biggest customer base. Food companies with growing revenue have larger labelling budgets and are more open to investing in premium labelling or new solutions.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU beverage manufacturers. Shows the financial health of a major label-consuming industry.
sales_interest: Beverage company revenue trends. Growing turnover means more money for premium labels, new product launches, and packaging redesigns — all generating label orders.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU tobacco manufacturers.
sales_interest: Tobacco company finances. Stable revenue despite volume declines = value growth through premium SKUs, each needing new labels.

description: Monthly index tracking total revenue of EU pharmaceutical companies. Pharma is a high-margin sector with growing labelling requirements.
sales_interest: Pharma revenue growth supports investment in sophisticated labelling — track & trace, anti-counterfeit, multi-language booklet labels. Growing turnover = bigger label budgets.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU food manufacturers. Shows local food market strength.
sales_interest: Local food market health. Strong domestic sales = strong local label demand. Use to identify countries with growing domestic food markets as label sales targets.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU beverage manufacturers.
sales_interest: Local beverage market health. Country-level domestic beverage revenue trends help identify where local label demand is growing.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU tobacco manufacturers.
sales_interest: Domestic tobacco sales. Useful for understanding local regulatory label demand from tobacco companies.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic-market revenue of EU pharmaceutical companies.
sales_interest: Local pharma market revenue. Growing domestic pharma sales = growing local demand for pharma labelling solutions.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU food manufacturers. Exported food needs labels that comply with destination-country regulations.
sales_interest: Food export trends. Growing food exports create demand for multi-language and destination-compliant labels — opportunity for label solutions that handle regulatory complexity.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU beverage manufacturers. Exported beverages need country-specific labels.
sales_interest: Beverage export revenue. Growing exports = more label SKUs needed per product (different languages, regulations per market). Complexity drives label revenue.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU tobacco manufacturers.
sales_interest: Tobacco export trends. Exports require market-specific health warnings and label designs — driving label variety and volume.

description: Monthly index tracking export revenue of EU pharmaceutical companies.
sales_interest: Pharma exports. Each export market requires specific label compliance (language, serialisation). Growing pharma exports = more complex, higher-value labelling needs.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of food products. Shows whether food manufacturers have pricing power or face deflationary pressure.
sales_interest: Food pricing power. If food companies can raise prices, they have healthier margins and more budget for labelling. Deflation squeezes their budgets and puts price pressure on label suppliers.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of beverages.
sales_interest: Beverage pricing power. Rising beverage prices = healthier margins for our customers and more willingness to invest in premium labels.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of tobacco products.
sales_interest: Tobacco pricing. Tobacco companies typically have strong pricing power due to regulatory barriers — supportive of stable label budgets.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of HPC products. Shows pricing trends in the premium personal care and cleaning sectors.
sales_interest: HPC pricing power. Premium brands with rising prices invest heavily in packaging differentiation — opportunity for premium label upsells.

description: Monthly index tracking factory-gate selling prices of pharmaceutical products.
sales_interest: Pharma pricing. Pharma has strong pricing power — labelling cost is a tiny fraction of product value. Use to show pharma customers that premium labelling is a negligible cost vs product value.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of food products. Reflects local food inflation or deflation.
sales_interest: Local food prices. Food inflation supports label price increases — if their products cost more, your label cost increase is easier to absorb.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of beverages.
sales_interest: Local beverage prices. Track for the same logic — customer pricing power supports your pricing power.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of tobacco products.
sales_interest: Domestic tobacco pricing. Typically rising due to excise taxes — very supportive of label budgets.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of HPC products.
sales_interest: Local HPC prices. Premiumisation trends (higher prices per unit) correlate with demand for more premium labels.

description: Monthly index tracking domestic selling prices of pharmaceutical products.
sales_interest: Local pharma pricing. Typically high and stable — confirms that label cost is negligible for pharma customers.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU food products. Reflects competitiveness of EU food exports.
sales_interest: Food export prices. If EU food becomes more expensive abroad, export volumes may fall, reducing label demand for export packaging.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU beverages.
sales_interest: Beverage export prices. Tracks EU beverage competitiveness in global markets — affects export label volume demand.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU tobacco products.
sales_interest: Tobacco export pricing. Supports understanding of export-related label demand dynamics.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU HPC products.
sales_interest: HPC export prices. EU cosmetics are exported globally — premium export pricing supports demand for premium label solutions.

description: Monthly index tracking export selling prices of EU pharmaceutical products.
sales_interest: Pharma export pricing. EU pharma exports are high-value — each product needs compliant labels for destination markets.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported food products entering the EU. Rising food imports can signal growing consumer demand not met by domestic production.
sales_interest: Imported food costs. Growing food imports into the EU mean more foreign products needing EU-compliant labels — relabelling opportunity.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported beverages entering the EU.
sales_interest: Imported beverage costs. Imported drinks often need local-market labels — opportunity for relabelling and compliance label services.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported tobacco products entering the EU.
sales_interest: Imported tobacco costs. Strict EU tobacco labelling regulations mean imports always need compliant local labels.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported HPC products entering the EU.
sales_interest: Imported HPC costs. Foreign cosmetics entering the EU need EU-compliant labels — relabelling services opportunity.

description: Monthly index tracking the cost of imported pharmaceutical products entering the EU.
sales_interest: Imported pharma costs. All imported medicines need EU-compliant, serialised labels — high-value labelling opportunity.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in EU food manufacturing. A proxy for how busy food factories are — more hours means more products being made and labelled.
sales_interest: Food factory activity. More hours worked = more production runs = more labels consumed. Direct leading indicator of label order volumes from food customers.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in EU beverage manufacturing.
sales_interest: Beverage factory activity. Rising hours = filling lines running longer = more label rolls being consumed. Track for demand forecasting.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in EU tobacco manufacturing.
sales_interest: Tobacco factory activity. Steady labour input confirms stable production and predictable label demand.

description: Monthly index tracking total hours worked in EU pharmaceutical manufacturing.
sales_interest: Pharma factory activity. Rising hours worked = more medicines being produced = more labels and packaging needed.

description: Monthly survey of EU food industry managers on recent production, order books, and expectations. Above zero = optimistic, below = pessimistic.
sales_interest: Food industry sentiment. Confident food manufacturers invest in new products and packaging. Low confidence may signal tighter purchasing budgets — adjust your pitch accordingly.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from EU beverage industry managers.
sales_interest: Beverage industry sentiment. Optimistic beverage companies launch new products and invest in premium packaging — driving label demand.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from EU tobacco industry managers.
sales_interest: Tobacco industry sentiment. Useful for gauging investment appetite in packaging innovation and label changes.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from EU HPC/cosmetics industry managers.
sales_interest: HPC industry sentiment. Confident cosmetics companies invest in packaging redesigns and premium labelling — high-value opportunities.

description: Monthly confidence indicator from EU pharmaceutical industry managers.
sales_interest: Pharma industry sentiment. Positive outlook supports investment in new products, each requiring label design and procurement.

description: Monthly index tracking overall retail sales revenue across the EU (excluding cars). The broadest measure of consumer spending on goods that carry labels.
sales_interest: The ultimate demand driver. If consumers are buying more products at retail, more products are being labelled. Track as the macro indicator for overall label market health.

code_description: Retail sale of food, beverages and tobacco
description: Monthly index tracking retail sales of food, beverages, and tobacco products. The largest retail segment for labelled goods.
sales_interest: Food retail is the largest single driver of label demand. Track to benchmark overall food label market conditions and to pitch solutions to food brand owners.

code_description: Dispensing chemist, medical goods, cosmetics, toiletries
description: Monthly index tracking retail sales at pharmacies, health stores, and cosmetics shops. All products sold here are heavily labelled.
sales_interest: HPC and pharma retail sales. Growing retail spending in this segment = growing demand for premium health, beauty, and pharma labels.

description: Monthly index tracking non-food retail sales (excluding fuel). Includes electronics, clothing, homeware — all sectors using labels for branding and logistics.
sales_interest: Broad non-food retail. Growing sales across non-food categories drives label demand for product labelling, promotional stickers, and logistics labels.

description: Monthly index tracking sales at supermarkets and hypermarkets (stores selling mainly food but also general merchandise). The primary channel for labelled consumer goods.
sales_interest: Supermarket sales — the main shelf where labelled products end up. Growing supermarket turnover directly translates to more labelled products moving through the supply chain.

description: Monthly index tracking output of the EU transportation and storage sector. Logistics activity drives demand for shipping labels, warehouse labels, and tracking labels.
sales_interest: Overall logistics activity. More goods being moved and stored = more shipping labels, pallet labels, and warehouse inventory labels consumed.

description: Monthly index tracking output of EU road and rail freight services. Every shipment typically requires transport labels.
sales_interest: Road and rail freight volume. More freight = more transport labels, hazardous goods labels, and consignment labels.

description: Monthly index tracking output of EU warehousing and logistics support services. Warehouses are major consumers of barcode labels, shelf labels, and inventory tracking labels.
sales_interest: Warehouse activity. More warehousing = more inventory labels, bin labels, and barcode labels. Track especially with e-commerce fulfilment growth.

description: Monthly index tracking output of EU postal and courier services. Driven by e-commerce, every parcel carries at least one shipping label.
sales_interest: E-commerce parcel volume proxy. Every online order generates a shipping label. This is one of the fastest-growing label segments — track to pitch thermal shipping labels and linerless solutions.

code_description: Retail sale of food, beverages and tobacco
description: Monthly survey of EU food retailers on recent sales, stock levels, and expectations. Above zero = optimistic. Leading indicator of food retail trends.
sales_interest: Food retailer confidence. If food retailers are optimistic, they are likely ordering more stock — which means more labelled products flowing through the supply chain.

description: Monthly survey of EU non-food retailers on business conditions and expectations.
sales_interest: Non-food retailer confidence. Optimistic retailers invest in promotions and new product ranges — each generating label demand.

description: Monthly survey of EU transport and logistics companies on business activity and outlook. Above zero = optimistic. Forward-looking indicator of logistics demand.
sales_interest: Logistics confidence. Optimistic logistics companies expect more parcels and freight — translating to more shipping and tracking labels. Leading indicator for logistics label demand.
